subject: Chemistry
solution:
The given compound not dissolve in cold NaHCO3 and NaOH solution, means  that does not have carboxylic acid group or phenol group.  But the compounds will dissolve in hot NaOH solution means they contain ester functional group.

The compounds 2,3,7 contain ester functional group.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Peptide linkage is also known as peptide bond. It is an amide formed between −COOH and −NH2 group by elimination of a water molecule. It is represented as
-CO-NH-.

Number of peptide linkages =(Total amino acids -1)

Now, given that short peptide on hydrolysis give seven amino acids.
=7-1=6

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The given reaction is exothermic reaction, hence, decreasing temperature is the favourable condition. The change in gaseous moles of products and reactants is negative, hence, increasing pressure is the favourable condition. Addition of reactants and removal of products are the favourable conditions. Catalyst only increase the speed of the reaction.

The yield of SO3 at equilibrium will be due to :
B. Increasing pressure
C. Adding more SO2
D. Adding more O2

solution:
Antihistamines do not affect the secretion of acid in stomach, the reason is that antiallergic and antacid drugs work on different receptors.

So, both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Bond strength is inversely proportional to the bond length of E - H bond of hydrides of 16th group hydrides. The bond length increases down the group. Hence, bond dissociation enthalpy decreases down the group.

Hence, bond dissociation energy of E-H bond in hydrides of group 16 follows the order

H2O > H2 S > H2Se > H2Te.

subject: Chemistry
options: A – II, B – I, C – III, D – IV
A – I, B – II, C – III, D – IV
A – II, B – I, C – IV, D – III
A – I, B– II, C – IV, D – III
solution:
Ni is in zero oxidation state in [Ni(CO)4]. So, the electronic configuration of Ni is 3d8 4s2. As CO is a strong ligand, it pushes all the electrons in the 3d orbital, therefore the hybridisation of [Ni(CO)4] is sp3 and it has tetrahedral geometry. It is diamagnetic due to the absence of unpaired electrons.

CuNH342+ ion, oxidation state of Cu is +2 and its valence shell electronic configuration is 3d9.  So, there would be a rearrangement of electrons in Cu2+ because of the NH3 strong field ligand . And the last electron in the d-orbital would be out waiting for the N's electrons to fill up first. So, the 4 electron pairs from N would be in one 3d, one 4s, & two 4p orbitals and in the third place of 4p orbital, the e- from 3d would take place. So, you can say the hybridisation here would be dsp2.

FeNH362+ ion, oxidation state of Fe is +2 and its valence shell electronic configuration is 3d6. NH3 is strong field ligand with complex. So, no pairing will be there. So, you can say the hybridisation here would be d2sp3.

 

FeH2O62+ ion, oxidation state of Fe is +2 and its valence shell electronic configuration is 3d6. There are 4 unpaired electrons in 3d orbital.  So, you can say the hybridisation here would be sp3d2.

So, correct order of matching is given in the below table:

List I
(Complexes)		 List II
(Hybridisation)
Ni(CO)4 sp3
CuNH342+ dsp2
FeNH362+ d2sp3
FeH2O62+ sp3d2

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In facial isomer, three Cl atoms present on one face of the octahedron. Similarly, three ammonia molecules are present on one face of the octahedron. All 3 same ligands have 90° bond angle between them.

The Cl-Co-Cl bond angle in above octahedral complex is 90°

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In the acidic medium, MnO4- convert to Mn2+ and I- convert to I2.

2MnO4-+10I-+16H+→2Mn2++5I2+8H2O

In neutral/faintly alkaline solution, MnO4- convert to MnO2 and I- convert to IO3-.

2MnO4-+I-+H2O→2MnO2+2OH-+IO3-

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The net cell reaction can be written as follows,

X+Y2+→Y+X2+

Ecell0=Ecathode0-Eanode0(Both are SRP values)

ECell0=0.36--2.36=2.72 V

Now, the Nernst equation is,

Ecell=Ecell0-0.06nlogX2+Y2+

n=The number of electrons involved in the net reaction.

ECell=2.72-0.062log0.0010.01

=2.72+0.03=2.75 V

=275×10-2 V

subject: Chemistry
options: Water has high amount of fluoride compounds
solution:
The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically.

Clean water has BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas polluted water has a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

So, the pond has very clean water.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Maximum resonating structure are possible in (c) structure. In (a) structure the carbocation is stabilised by lone pair on nitrogen as well as two double bonds in the ring. In (b) and (d) structure lone pair of nitrogen is not involved in conjugation to stabilise positive charge. So, NH2 group will show -I effect.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Electron-withdrawing groups increase the acidic nature of the phenol. Nitro group is electron withdrawing group, hence it is the more acidic compound among the given phenols.  

Electron-donating groups decrease the acidic nature where methoxy group is more electron donating, hence less acidic than the iso-propyl group which donates electrons by hyperconjugation effect.

∴ Order of acidic strength is (c) > (a) > (d) > (b)

The order of pKa values are (b) > (d) >(a) > (c).

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Chloroform (b.p 334 K) and aniline (b.p. 457 K) are easily separated by the technique of distillation.

One of the technological applications of fractional distillation is to separate different fractions of crude oil in the petroleum industry.

Aniline is separated by steam distillation technique from aniline – water mixture.

When an organic compound is present in an aqueous medium, it is separated by shaking it with an organic solvent in which it is more soluble than in water.

Hence, correct matching is given by the below table:

List I (Mixture) List II
(Separation
Technique)
CHCl3+C6H5NH2 Distillation
C6H14+C5H12 Fractional
distillation
C6H5NH2+H2O Steam distillation
Organic compound in H2O Differential
extraction

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
solution:
In Electrolytic refining, the pure metal is used as cathode and impure metal is used as anode. Electrolytic refining of aluminium is called Hall-Heroult process. 

Na3AlF6 is added during electrolysis of Al2O3 to lower the melting point and increase conductivity.
Reactions at cathode and anode are as follows:
Cathode: Al3+(melt) + 3e- → Al(l)

Anode: C(s) + O2- (melt) → CO(g) + 2e-C(s) + 2O2- (melt) → CO2  (g) + 4e-

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The relative reactivity of alkyl halides in the SN1 reaction depends on the stability of carbocation. Electron-withdrawing groups decrease the stability and electron-releasing groups increase the stability of carbocations. -NO2 group is an electron-withdrawing group by -M effect. -OMe is electron-releasing groups by +M effect. Hence, the overall order is 

∴ Reactivity order (b) > (d) > (c) > (a)

subject: Chemistry
solution: The nitro group on the benzene ring is reduced to amine group with metal and acid. Now, the methyl and nitro groups present on the benzene ring are ortho-para directing groups and amine group is the strong activator. Hence, bromine water reacts with respect to amino group. The amino group on the benzene ring undergoes diazotization with nitrous acid. The  diazonium part reduces to benzene with hypophosphorous acid.

subject: Chemistry
options: A is false but R is true
A is true but R is false
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
solution:
Clemmensen reduction: The carbonyl group of aldehydes and ketones is reduced to CH2 group on treatment with zinc-amalgam and concentrated hydrochloric acid.

The acid sensitive alcohol group reacts with HCl, hence Clemmenson reduction is not suitable for above conversion.

So, assertion is false but reason is true.

As per Fajan's rule, Be cations has a higher charge and smaller size, as a result, have a higher charge density and covalent nature.

Ca, Mg, K are ionic compounds.

Hence, chlorides of Be will be more soluble in organic solvent.

Lithium nitrate when decomposed, the product formed are Li2O, NO2 and O2. The remaining alkali metal nitrates decomposes to nitrite of metals and oxygen gas. It is due to the lithium monoxide is stable than other monoxides. 

4LiNO3⟶Δ2Li2O+4NO2+O2

The wave function (Ψ) of 2s is given by,

Ψ2s=122π1a01/22-ra0e-r/2a0

At r = r0, radial node is formed.

Ψ2s2 = Probability density of finding an electron

At node, Ψ2s2 = 0 (∵Probability of finding an electron is zero at the node)

Ψ2s2 =122π1a01/22-ra0e-r/2a02

⇒ 0 =18π1a0 2-r0a02e-2r0/a0

⇒ 2 - r0a0 = 0

⇒ 2 = r0a0

⇒ 2a0 = r0

So, the correct option is D.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The maximum number of electrons that could be present in a shell is given by the rule 2n2.

Where n is the shell number.

The first shell K shell can accommodate a maximum of 2 electrons (2 × 12 = 2).

The second shell L shell can accommodate a maximum of 8 electrons (2 × 22 = 8).

Likewise, the 4th shell named N can accommodate a maximum of 32 electrons (2 × 42 = 2 × 16 = 32).

solution:
We know the binomial expansion of a+bn is,

a+bn=C0nanb0+C1nan-1b1+C2nan-2b2+......+Cnna0bn

Using the above expansion,

x=83+1313=C0138313+C1138312131+…

Let x'=83-1313=C0138313-C1138312131+…

So, x-x'=2C113·8312131+C3138310·133…

Therefore, x-x' is an even integer, hence x is even

Now, y=72+99=C09729+C1972891+C2972792+....Now let,y'=(72-9)9=C09(72)9-C19(72)8(9)1+C2972792

So, y-y'=2C1972891+C3972693+…

y-y'= Even integer hence y is even

solution:

x150=12⇒x=1250

y150=18⇒y=1850

So,

x+y=1250+1850

⇒x+y25=125025+185025

⇒x+y25=1231612225+1822525

⇒x+y25=17281612225+325-12525

⇒x+y25=1725+31612225+325-12525

So, remainder is

31612225-1=335×3×14425-1

=25+25×3×14425-1

=25+25×3×150-625-1

=25×3×-625-1

=25+7×3×-625-1

=21×-625-1

=25-4×-625-1

=-4×-625-1

=24-1=23

Hence, the remainder is 23

solution:

Function fx=xx,x≠01,x=0,   gx=sinx+1x+1,x≠-11,x=-1 and hx=2x-fx

Taking L.H.L, we take x=1-h

limh→0-g(h(1-h-1))

⇒limh→0-g2-h--h-h

⇒limh→0-g(2(-1)+1)

⇒limh→0-g-1=1

Now taking R.H.L, we take x=1+h

limh→0+g(h(1+h-1))

=limh→0+g2h-f(h)

=limh→0+g(0-1)

⇒limh→0+1=1

solution:
Given,

PT=aP+(a-1)I......(1)

Taking transpose on both sides we get,

PTT=aP+(a-1)IT

⇒P=aPT+(a-1)I.......(2)

Subtract equation (1) and (2),

⇒PT-P=aP-PT

⇒P=PT as a≠-1

From equation (1),

P=aP+(a-1)I

⇒P=-I

Taking adjoint on both sides,

⇒Adj P=Adj(-I)

⇒Adj P=Adj (-I)=-I2

⇒|AdjP|=1

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

ax2+2bx+cy=0 and d2+2ex+fy=0 intersect on the line y=1,

And a,b,c are in G.P.

So, b2=ac

Now putting the value of b in ax2+2bx+cy=0and taking y=1 we get,

ax2+2bx+c=0

⇒ax2+2acx+c=0 ∵b2=ac

⇒(xa+c)2=0

⇒x=-ca and x2=ca .......1

Now, putting the value of x & x2 in dx2+2ex+fy=0 and taking y=1 we get,

⇒dca+2e-ca+f=0

⇒dca+f=2eca

⇒da+fc=2e1ac

⇒da+fc=2eb  as b=ac

∴da,eb,fcare in A.P.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Let a tangent on parabola y2=16x be,

y=mx+4 m

It is also common to x2+y2=8

If 4m2=81+m2

⇒16m2=8(1+m2)

⇒m4+m2-2=0⇒(m2+2)(m2-1)=0

⇒m=±1

Taking one of the tangents y=x+4

⇒x-y+4=0

Tangent to x2+y2=8 is xx1+yy1-8=0

∴x11=y11=-84

⇒Q(-2,2)

Tangent to y2=16x is yy1=8(x+x1)

⇒8x-yy1+8x1=0

∴81=-y1-1=8x14

⇒y1=8, x1=4

∴R(4,8)

Using the distance formula between two points,

QR=(4+2)2+(8-2)2=62

∴(QR)2=622=72

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Let a=2,4,6,...,100 and b=1,3,5,...,99

Let

a+b=23λ+2

If λ=1, then

a+b=25

So, 1,24,3,22,....,23,2

Total ordered pairs=12

If λ=2, then

a+b=48

No ordered pair possible.

If λ=3, then

a+b=71

So,

1,70, 3,68, 5,66,....,61,10, 63,8, 65,6, 67,4, 69,2

Total 35 ordered pairs.

If λ=5, then

a+b=117

17,100, 19,98, ..., 99,18

Total 42 ordered pairs.

If λ=7, then

a+b=163

So,

63,100, 65,63,.... 99,64

Total 19 pairs.

No further case possible.

So, required number is

=12+35+42+19=108

subject: Mathematics
solution:
To find the number of 7 digit odd number formed by using the seven digits 1,2,2,2,3,3,5

We will take following cases,

Case 1- fixing 1 as unit place,

So total number of ways will be 6!3!2! as 2 is repeating three times and 3 is repeating two times

Case 2- fixing 3 unit place,

So total number of ways will be 6!3!

Case 3- fixing 5 as unit place,

So total number of ways will be 6!3!2!

Now adding all the cases we will get, total number of ways as 6!3!2!+6!3!+6!3!2!=60+120+60=240

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

A bag contains six balls of different colours, two balls are drawn in succession with replacement,

The probability that both the balls are of the same colour will be p=636

Now Next four balls are drawn in succession with replacement and the probability that exactly three balls are of the same colours is q,

So, probability will be q=C16×C15×C1464=1201296=554

Now the ratio of p & q will be pq=95

Now, on comparing with pq=mn⇒m+n=14

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

Quadratic equation x2-5αx+1=0  and x2-αx-5=0 have a common real root,

So, taking k to be common root then equation becomes,

k2-5αk+1=0 ......1

k2-αk-5=0 .....2

Now subtraction above equations we get,

k=64α

Now putting the value of k in equation 1 we get,

64α2-5α64α+1=0 

⇒64α2-264=0 

⇒3616α2=264

⇒α2=926

⇒α=32×13

Now comparing with given value 32β we get, β=13

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

a3,b3,c3 be in A.P.

So, a3+c3=2b3 ........1

Also given logab, logca & logbc  are in G.P.

So, logbloga·logclogb=logalogc2

⇒(loga)3=(logc)3⇒a=c  ..........(2)

Now from equation 1 & 2 we get, a=b=c

Now, T1=a+4b+c3=2a and d=a-8b+c10=-6a10=-35a

∴S20=2024a+19-35a

⇒S20=1020a-57a5

⇒S20=-74a

⇒-444=-74a⇒a=6

Hence, abc=63=216

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

Two A.P's  as

3,7,11,15,... 

First terma1=3

Common differenced1=4

And,

1,6,11,16,...,

First terma2=1

Common difference d2=5

Now, common difference of the series of the common terms of the given A.P's is d=LCMd1,d2=LCM4,5=20

Now, series of common term is 

11,31,51,71,...

So, 8th common term appearing in the both the series is

a8=11+8-1d

⇒a8=11+7×20=151

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

a1  be any natural number 

So, a1,a1+1,a1+2,….,a1+99 are values of ai or observation.

Now mean of the observation is given by,

x¯=a1+a1+1+a1+2+….+a1+99100

⇒x¯=100a1+(1+2+…..+99)100

⇒x¯=a1+99×1002×100

⇒x¯=a1+992

Mean deviation about mean=∑i=1100xi-x¯100

=2992+972+952+….+12100

=1+3+….+99100

=502[1+99]100

=25

So, it is true for every natural no. ' a1 ' as there is no term of a1 in mean deviation.

a,b,c lies on it,

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

A plane passes through the points (-1,k,0),(2,k,-1), (1,1,2) and is parallel to the line x-11=2y+12 =z+1-1,

So, on rearranging x-11=2y+12=z+1-1 we get,

⇒x-11=y+121=z+1-1

Now given point : A(-1,k,0),B(2,k,-1),C(1,1,2) 

So, CA→=-2i^+(k-1)j^-2k^, 

CB→=i^+(k-1)j^-3k^

And CA→×CB→=i^j^k^-2k-1-21k-1-3

⇒CA→×CB→=i^(-3k+3+2k-2)-j^(6+2)+k^(-2k+2-k+1)

⇒CA→×CB→=i^(-3k+3+2k-2)-j^(6+2)+k^(-2k+2-k+1)

⇒CA→×CB→=(1-k)i^-8j^+(3-3k)k^

The line x-11=y+121=z+1-1 is perpendicular to normal vector.

Now using the condition of perpendicular vector we get, 

1(1-k)+1(-8)+(-1)(3-3k)=0

⇒1-k-8-3+3k=0

⇒2k=10⇒k=5

Hence, k2+1(k-1)(k-2)=264×3=136

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

x+3y-2z-2=0=x-y+2z

Now direction ratio is given by,

i^j^k^13-21-12=4i^-4j^-4k^

So, Equation of line will be x-24=y-3-4=z-1-4

⇒x-21=y-3-1=z-1-1=k

Let Q be (5,3,8) and foot of ⊥ from Q on this line be R

Now, R≡(k+2,-k+3,-k+1)

So, direction ratio of  QR will be (k-3,-k,-k-7),

Now using the perpendicular condition we get,

(1)(k-3)+(-1)(-k)+(-1)(-k-7)=0

⇒k=-43

So, α2=1332+432+1732=4749

⇒3α2=158

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

a→=λi^+2j^-3k^

b→=i^-λj^+2k^

Also given,

((a→+b→)×(a→×b→))×(a→-b→)=8i^-40j^-24k^

⇒(b→-a→)×((a→+b→)×(a→×b→))=8i^-40j^-24k^

Now using vector triple product,

A→×(B→×C→)=(A→.C→)B→-(A→.B→)C→ we get,

⇒((b→-a→)·(a→×b→))(a→+b→)-((b→-a→)·(a→+b→))(a→×b→)=8i^-40j^-24k^

⇒0+((a→-b→)·(a→+b→))(a→×b→)=8i^-40j^-24k^.....(1)

((a→-b→)·(a→+b→))=λ-1i^+2+λj^-5k^.λ+1i^+2-λj^-k^

⇒((a→-b→)·(a→+b→))=8

From equation (1) we get,

⇒8(a→×b→)=8i^-40j^-24k^

Now, a→×b→=i^j^k^λ2-31-λ2

=(4-3λ)i^-(2λ+3)j^+-λ2-2k^

⇒λ=1

∴a→=i^+2j^-3k^

b→=i^-j^+2k^

⇒a→+b→=2i^+j^-k^,a→-b→=3j^-5k^

⇒(a→+b→)×(a→-b→)=i^j^k^21-103-5=2i^+10j^+6k^

⇒(a→+b→)×(a→-b→)=4+100+36

Now,

λ(a→+b→)×(a→-b→)2=λ2(a→+b→)×(a→-b→)2

∴ Required answer =4+100+36=140

solution:

⇒b→·c→=-48

subject: Physics
solution:
Attenuation is the loss of signal strength in networking cables or connections.

Transducers are used in electronic communications systems to convert signals of various physical forms to electronic signals, and vice versa.

Demodulation is defined as extracting the original information-carrying signal from a modulated carrier wave.

Repeaters are used to extend transmissions so that the signal can cover longer distances or be received on the other side of an obstruction.

subject: Physics
solution:
Given:

E→=2x2i^-4yj^+6k^

For top and bottom faces, as value of x=0 & y=0, electric field becomes constant and therefore net flux through these two faces will be zero(ϕz=0).

Now, Ex=2x2, Ey=-4y and Ez=6.

Change in net flux through face at x=1 and at x=0 parallel to y-z plane can written as, ϕx=2×12×2×3-0=12.

Similarly, net flux through face at y=2 and y=0 parallel to x-z plane can be written as, ϕy=-4×2×1×3-0=-24

ϕnet=12-24=-12

Using Gauss's law,

ϕnet=qε0

⇒q=12ε0
